_schema.purpose: Net-new reference input for the Investment-Case Benchmark (Phase 1 spec §7 item 1). Public model API list prices, USD per MILLION tokens (input/output). TAIL does NOT hold this — it is the naive list-price floor the benchmark then adjusts with TAIL factors (packaging margin + repricing multiple). This file is the Layer-1 input; everything that makes the output credible comes from TAIL.
_schema.status: DRAFT — prices gathered via web search of public pricing pages 2026-06-18; VERIFY against official pages before Phase 3 build.
_schema.caveat: List/standard tier only. Batch (~50% off), cached input, and priority tiers are noted where known but not modelled in v1.

claude_opus_4_8.note: Flagship, released 2026-05-28. Fast Mode $10/$50. 1M context no surcharge.

gpt_5_line_pre_apr2026.note: GPT-5 line standard pricing BEFORE the 2026-04-23 increase. Retained as the real-world repricing proof point — see repricing_note.

gemini_2_5_pro.note: Cheapest frontier-class input among the majors.

deepseek_v4_flash.note: Current cheapest flagship-class API; cache hit ~98% cheaper.

mistral_large_3.note: Cheapest flagship-class input; output est. — VERIFY.

lfm2_24b_together.note: Absolute-cheapest production model 2026-06; output est. — VERIFY. Floor reference.

repricing_note._doc: Real-world proof that the repricing thesis (Phase 1 spec §3b) is not hypothetical: OpenAI DOUBLED the GPT-5 line on 2026-04-23 with GPT-5.5 — input $2.50→$5.00, output $15→$30. Use as the concrete corroboration that current AI pricing is not stable and can move sharply upward. Anchor for the 'price won't hold' line.
repricing_note.src: OpenAI GPT-5.5 release pricing, 2026-04-23 (public pricing page / OpenRouter cost analysis).
